source_paper_title: On the Convex Formulations of Robust Markov Decision Processes
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Grand_Cl_ment_2025, title={On the Convex Formulations of Robust Markov Decision Processes}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0284}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0284}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Grand-Clément, Julien and Petrik, Marek}, year={2025}, month=aug, pages={1681–1706} }
context:
Section 1 (Introduction), page 2; reiterated in Section 7 (Conclusion), page 22.

Surprisingly, there is no known convex optimization formulation for RMDPs, analogous to the linear programming formulation of MDPs.

We assert that this is an important problem in the field of RMDPs too.

First, it may be possible to obtain exact formulations for RMDPs, not based on regularization, for specific uncertainty sets such as \(\ell_p\)-balls or \(\phi\)-divergence balls around nominal transitions.
title: Derive an exact tractable convex program for robust Markov decision processes
background_latex:
A discounted robust Markov decision process (RMDP) is specified by finite sets of states \(S\) and actions \(A\), nonnegative rewards \(r_{sa}\in\mathbb{R}_+\) for \((s,a)\in S\times A\), a discount factor \(\gamma\in(0,1)\), an initial distribution \(\alpha\in\Delta(S)\), and an uncertainty set \(\mathcal{U}\subseteq (\Delta(S))^{S\times A}\) of plausible transition kernels \(P=(P_{sas'})\). A stationary policy is \(\pi\in\Pi:=(\Delta(A))^S\). For a fixed transition kernel \(P\), the discounted return of \(\pi\) is
\[
R(\pi,P)=\mathbb{E}_{\pi,P}\Big[\sum_{t=0}^\infty \gamma^t\, r_{S_tA_t}\ \Big|\ S_0\sim\alpha\Big].
\]
The RMDP objective is the max–min problem
\[
\max_{\pi\in\Pi}\ \min_{P\in\mathcal{U}}\ R(\pi,P).
\]
A common tractable structure is rectangularity of \(\mathcal{U}\). Under sa-rectangularity one has \(\mathcal{U}=\prod_{(s,a)\in S\times A} \mathcal{U}_{sa}\) with \(\mathcal{U}_{sa}\subseteq\Delta(S)\) convex and compact, and the robust Bellman operator \(T:\mathbb{R}^S\to\mathbb{R}^S\) is
\[
T(v)_s = \max_{a\in A}\ \min_{p\in\mathcal{U}_{sa}}\Big\{ r_{sa}+\gamma\, p^\top v\Big\},\qquad s\in S.
\]
The fixed point \(v^\star\) of \(T\) yields an optimal robust value function and an optimal stationary policy. Unlike the nominal MDP case (where a linear program exists), the maps \(v\mapsto T(v)_s\) need not be convex or concave, so the feasible sets \(\{v\in\mathbb{R}^S: v\ge T(v)\}\) or \(\{v\in\mathbb{R}^S: v\le T(v)\}\) are not known to admit a straightforward convex description in general.
problem_definition_latex:
Find conditions and a polynomial-size convex optimization formulation (e.g., conic, geometric, or other convex program) whose optimal solutions encode an optimal robust stationary policy and the optimal robust value function for the discounted RMDP
\[
\max_{\pi\in\Pi}\ \min_{P\in\mathcal{U}}\ R(\pi,P),
\]
under classical rectangular uncertainty (in particular sa-rectangular or s-rectangular \(\mathcal{U}\)), without introducing an approximation scheme based on regularization (i.e., an exact convex reformulation of the original RMDP rather than a sequence of convex programs converging to it).

Find conditions and a polynomial-size convex optimization formulation (e.g., conic, geometric, or other convex program) whose optimal solutions encode an optimal robust stationary policy and the optimal robust value function for the discounted RMDP
\[
\max_{\pi\in\Pi}\ \min_{P\in\mathcal{U}}\ R(\pi,P),
\]
under classical rectangular uncertainty (in particular sa-rectangular or s-rectangular \(\mathcal{U}\)), without introducing an approximation scheme based on regularization (i.e., an exact convex reformulation of the original RMDP rather than a sequence of convex programs converging to it).
location_in_paper: Section 1 (Introduction), page 2; reiterated in Section 7 (Conclusion), page 22.
open_problem_quote:
Surprisingly, there is no known convex optimization formulation for RMDPs, analogous to the linear programming formulation of MDPs.

We assert that this is an important problem in the field of RMDPs too.

First, it may be possible to obtain exact formulations for RMDPs, not based on regularization, for specific uncertainty sets such as \(\ell_p\)-balls or \(\phi\)-divergence balls around nominal transitions.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for existence/construction of an exact polynomial-size convex reformulation in broad generality (or broad classes of uncertainty sets), which is not naturally falsified by finite counterexamples; failure may depend on complexity-theoretic obstructions rather than a small explicit instance.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: For specific uncertainty sets (e.g., \(\ell_p\)-balls or divergence balls), one can represent candidate reformulations or variable changes and test them computationally by comparing their optimal value against robust value iteration on randomly generated small RMDPs, giving an automatic fitness signal. However, the space of possible exact convexifications is very open-ended and success would likely be limited to discovering promising transformations or formulations for special cases rather than resolving the general problem.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
No forward-citation block was provided, so the review is grounded in the original paper’s framing and the classical robust MDP literature it cites. The Grand-Cl\'ement–Petrik paper establishes that the main obstruction to an LP-like convex formulation is the nonconvex/nonconcave nature of the robust Bellman map \(v\mapsto T(v)_s\) under rectangular uncertainty, even though \(T\) is a monotone \(\ell_\infty\)-contraction and minimax duality holds. Their key contribution is an approximate convex formulation: they introduce an entropically regularized robust Bellman operator \(\tilde T\) and, after an exponential change of variables, obtain polynomial-size convex (often conic) programs for common uncertainty sets (polyhedral, ellipsoidal, relative-entropy), with solutions converging to the true robust solution as the regularization parameter tends to zero.

Relative to the open problem as stated here—an exact, polynomial-size convex reformulation of the original discounted rectangular RMDP without regularization—these results are best viewed as evidence of “hidden convexity” in nearby regularized models rather than a complete solution. The foundational works of Iyengar (sa-rectangular) and Nilim–El Ghaoui (robust control with uncertain transitions) provide the contraction/fixed-point characterizations and tractable inner minimizations that any exact convex program would need to encode, while Wiesemann–Kuhn–Sim extend the tractable theory to s-rectangular sets and establish strong minimax duality. However, none of these works provide an exact convex feasible-set description for \(v\ge T(v)\) (or an equivalent occupancy-measure formulation) in the general rectangular case.

At present, the problem therefore remains open in full generality. Promising directions suggested by the existing literature include: (i) identifying special classes of uncertainty sets \(\mathcal U_{sa}\) for which \(v\mapsto \min_{p\in\mathcal U_{sa}} p^\top v\) admits a conic representable epigraph that interacts benignly with the outer \(\max_a\); (ii) exploiting alternative variables (e.g., robust occupancy measures or state-action potentials) that might convexify the max–min coupling; and (iii) understanding whether exact convexity is possible only with exponentially large coefficients/encodings (as in some stochastic-game formulations), which would clarify the limits of polynomial-size convex representability.

sources.title: Robust Dynamic Programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces discounted robust MDPs with sa-rectangular uncertainty and proves that the robust Bellman operator is a monotone \,\(\ell_\infty\)-contraction with an optimal stationary deterministic policy; provides robust value iteration and discusses obstacles to LP-like formulations (nonconvexity).
sources.connection: Defines the classical sa-rectangular model and robust Bellman operator \(T\) that the desired exact convex program would need to encode; also documents why naive LP generalizations fail, motivating the open problem.

sources.title: Robust control of Markov decision processes with uncertain transition matrices
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops robust MDPs with (sa-)rectangular uncertainty, establishes contraction and dynamic programming, and gives tractable inner minimizations for common uncertainty sets; includes a contraction-lemma style characterization of fixed points.
sources.connection: Provides foundational DP characterizations that any convex reformulation would leverage (e.g., fixed-point inequalities), and highlights that tractability comes from rectangularity but not necessarily convex representability of \(\{v\ge T(v)\}\).

sources.title: Robust Markov Decision Processes
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces s-rectangular uncertainty for RMDPs, proves strong minimax duality and existence of optimal stationary policies, and provides solution methods via robust dynamic programming; shows non-rectangular evaluation is NP-hard.
sources.connection: Extends the open problem’s scope to s-rectangular sets and supplies minimax/duality structure that suggests hidden convexity, yet does not yield a polynomial-size exact convex program.

sources.title: Robust control and model misspecification
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies robust control with relative-entropy (multiplier) preferences leading to risk-sensitive/entropy-regularized Bellman equations that are convex after exponential transformations in certain formulations.
sources.connection: Conceptually related to the Grand-Clément–Petrik approach: entropic regularization plus exponential change of variables can reveal convexity, but this does not provide an exact reformulation of the unregularized rectangular RMDP.

sources.title: Linearly-solvable Markov decision problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows that adding KL control costs yields Bellman equations solvable via an exponential transform, leading to convex/linear formulations in transformed variables for certain MDP classes.
sources.connection: Provides the prototype for the entropic-regularization and exponential change-of-variables trick used by Grand-Clément–Petrik to obtain convex programs, but again only for a regularized (not exact robust) objective.
